name: [Full Name]
title: Software Engineer | AI Integration
remotePreference: Open to Remote, Hybrid, or Onsite
yearsOfExperience: 5+ years total software development. 3+ years hands-on AI/LLM production work.
summary: AI Engineer with 5 plus years of software development experience and deep hands-on expertise building production AI applications using Large Language Models. Currently at Frontier Communications building LLM-powered features on Google Cloud Platform with Vertex AI and Gemini. Based in McKinney, TX. Skilled in multi-model orchestration, RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, and prompt engineering. Strong full stack background with Java Spring Boot backend and React TypeScript frontend. Experienced deploying AI systems on GCP and AWS with Kubernetes and Docker. IBM certified in Prompt Engineering. Oracle AI Foundations certified. AWS Solutions Architect certified.

aiAndLLMs: Multi-Model Orchestration — routing queries to the right model by complexity
Prompt Engineering — chain-of-thought, few-shot, system prompts, output formatting
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) — Pinecone, embeddings, semantic search
Agentic Workflows — tool use, function calling, autonomous task pipelines
AI Agents — multi-step reasoning, memory, planning
cloudAndMLOps: GCP — Vertex AI, Cloud Run, Cloud Storage, BigQuery

experience.title: AI/Software Engineer
experience.description: Frontier is a major US telecom provider. I work as a Java Full Stack Developer on their enterprise team, building LLM-powered features and microservices on GCP and AWS.
highlights: Integrated Vertex AI and Gemini 1.5 into enterprise telecom applications to automate customer-facing workflows that previously required human agents
Built a multi-model orchestration layer routing requests to Gemini Flash for simple queries and Gemini Pro for complex reasoning — reduced response latency by 40 percent while cutting cost
Implemented NLP-based SMS classification using LLM to automatically categorize and respond to customer text messages at scale
Designed and built RAG pipelines — chunked internal documentation, embedded with OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, stored in Pinecone, wired to Gemini for context-aware answers
Engineered production prompt templates with chain-of-thought reasoning, few-shot examples, and output format constraints for reliable LLM outputs
Built agentic workflows using function calling where AI queries internal APIs, looks up account info, and takes actions on behalf of customers
Developed RESTful APIs using Spring Boot, Spring MVC, Spring IOC, and Spring Security; integrated with Apache Kafka for asynchronous messaging
Integrated Spring Boot microservices with Apache Kafka and deployed in Docker containers on AWS EC2; managed AWS IAM, Lambda, S3, Glue, and CloudWatch
Built React TypeScript frontend features including real-time dashboards, SPA components using React Hooks, React-Router, and Redux
Implemented CI/CD pipelines using Docker, Jenkins, and GitLab; deployed microservices on Docker and Amazon EC2 using Kubernetes
Implemented caching with Redis; developed PL/SQL backend for Oracle database and managed migrations to DB2
Integrated Splunk for centralized log analysis; built dashboards and alerts for proactive system monitoring
Utilized Kafka with Spark Streaming for real-time data processing, storing results in PostgreSQL on Docker and Kubernetes
Achieved 90 percent plus code coverage through JUnit and Mockito unit and integration tests

experience.title: Software Engineer

highlights: Developed and optimized RESTful APIs with Spring Boot, reducing response times by 50 percent and improving throughput under heavy load
Designed data persistence layers using Hibernate and Spring Data JPA, eliminating manual query errors and streamlining CRUD operations
Automated test suites with JUnit and Selenium, driving a 35 percent reduction in bug-related incidents
Refactored legacy Java codebases, increasing processing speed by 20 percent through cleaner architecture
Architected microservices with Spring Cloud, improving system scalability by 40 percent
Implemented CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Docker, cutting deployment times by 60 percent
Deployed containerized services to AWS EKS via Kubernetes; enhanced observability using AWS CloudWatch and ELK Stack

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description: Software Engineer at Intellativ India building Java microservices and full stack web applications.
highlights: Built Java microservices using Spring Boot and Spring Cloud for scalable modular architecture
Exposed and consumed RESTful APIs with Spring MVC handling JSON/XML payloads and versioning
Managed data persistence via Hibernate and Spring Data JPA on MySQL and Oracle databases
Implemented asynchronous messaging with Apache Kafka and RabbitMQ for event-driven workflows
Containerized applications using Docker and orchestrated with Kubernetes on AWS EKS
Automated CI/CD pipelines in Jenkins and GitLab CI, cutting release times by 40 percent
Applied TDD with JUnit and Mockito achieving over 90 percent code coverage
Secured microservices using OAuth2/JWT and enforced role-based access control
Monitored performance with Prometheus and Grafana; centralized logging through ELK Stack

projects.name: Wealthix — AI-Powered Financial Intelligence Platform
projects.description: End-to-end AI financial platform. Built a Hybrid Router pattern that dynamically selects between Gemini 1.5 and Claude based on query type — fast financial lookups go to Gemini Flash, complex reasoning like tax optimization goes to Claude. Implemented a full RAG pipeline: PDF financial documents ingested, chunked, embedded with OpenAI embeddings, stored in Pinecone, retrieved by semantic similarity at query time. Deployed on AWS with load balancing and monitoring.
highlights: Multi-model orchestration: cost-optimized routing between Gemini Flash and Claude based on prompt complexity score
RAG pipeline end to end: ingest, chunk, embed, upsert, retrieve, inject into LLM context
Plaid integration for real bank account data — AI reasons over actual transaction history
Stripe payment integration with AI-generated invoice summaries

projects.name: AI Financial Co-Pilot — Automated Dispute Engine
projects.description: Agentic AI system for automated financial dispute resolution. The AI reads dispute documents using Claude 3.5, extracts key claims, cross-references transaction records, drafts resolution letters, and routes edge cases to human review. Built prompt engineering pipelines with structured output constraints so Claude always returns valid JSON that maps to dispute categories.
highlights: Claude 3.5 for document analysis — structured output with JSON schema enforcement
Agentic routing: AI decides whether to auto-resolve or escalate to human
Prompt pipeline with few-shot examples for consistent dispute classification
Real-time dashboard showing dispute status, AI confidence scores, and resolution timeline

projects.name: AI Call Screener — Voice AI Agent
projects.description: Real-time voice AI agent that intercepts recruiter phone calls and answers as me. Twilio Media Streams provides μ-law 8kHz audio via WebSocket. Deepgram nova-3 does streaming STT. Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 reasons from my resume context with RAG (Pinecone) for self-improvement after each call. ElevenLabs TTS generates the voice response. The system detects interruptions using AtomicReference and stops mid-sentence — exactly like a real person would. Conference bridge architecture enables seamless live call handoff between AI and human.
highlights: Real-time audio pipeline: Twilio WebSocket → Deepgram STT → Claude reasoning → ElevenLabs TTS → Twilio
Interrupt detection: AtomicReference tracks mid-speech caller interruptions, AI stops at sentence boundary
Conference bridge: Twilio conference rooms allow seamless AI ↔ human call handoff with no audio gap
Self-training: each call transcript ingested into Pinecone RAG so AI improves over time
Java virtual threads for non-blocking AI pipeline — each call runs concurrently without blocking

commonInterviewAnswers.tellMeAboutYourself: I am a Full Stack and AI Engineer with over 5 years of experience building enterprise microservices and LLM-powered features. My stack includes Java Spring Boot, React, and various cloud-native AI services.
commonInterviewAnswers.whyLeavingCurrentJob: I am looking for a role where AI is even more core to the product architecture.
commonInterviewAnswers.biggestStrength: I can take an AI concept from prototype to production end to end, handling both the LLM logic and the underlying engineering.
commonInterviewAnswers.biggestWeakness: I sometimes over-engineer solutions, but I am learning to prioritize minimum viable approaches.
commonInterviewAnswers.whereDoYouSeeYourselfIn5Years: Leading AI architecture for complex products.
commonInterviewAnswers.whyShouldWeHireYou: I bring production experience with LLM applications and a strong full-stack engineering background.
